title_zh: NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent 工具包助力 Claude Science 推动生命科学研究
title_en: NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit Brings Accelerated AI to Life Sciences Researchers in Claude Science
summary_zh: 随着生命科学进入计算规模时代，NVIDIA 十余年来构建了完整的 GPU 加速计算堆栈，包括硬件、框架、库、模型、微服务及领域专用工具，帮助研究人员进行更复杂的工作流程并加快迭代速度。本周，Anthropic 发布了 Claude Science，这是一款面向科学的 AI 工作台，结合了 NVIDIA 的技术生态。
summary_en: Life sciences has entered an era of computational scale, and NVIDIA has spent over a decade building a comprehensive GPU-accelerated computing stack—covering hardware, frameworks, libraries, models, microservices, and domain-specific tools—to help researchers run sophisticated workflows and iterate faster. This week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, an AI workbench for science, integrating NVIDIA's technologies.
why_it_matters_zh: 该工具包和工作台结合了深度学习与生命科学的优势，显著提升科研效率和计算能力，加速科学发现过程。
why_it_matters_en: The toolkit and workbench combine deep learning with life sciences expertise, significantly enhancing research efficiency and computational power to accelerate scientific discovery.

title_zh: NVIDIA 推理软件堆栈如何驱动最低代币成本
title_en: How NVIDIA’s Inference Software Stack Powers the Lowest Token Cost
summary_zh: 随着组织从AI试点转向生产AI工厂，基础设施的关注点从顶峰芯片规格转向每代币成本：即每美元、每瓦特及在要求延迟内可交付的有效代币数量。NVIDIA推理软件与GPU、CPU、网络和系统协同设计，并由广泛的开源生态系统支持，实现了成本效率的提升。
summary_en: As organizations transition from AI pilots to production AI factories, infrastructure priorities have shifted from peak chip specifications to cost per token: how many useful tokens can be delivered per dollar, per watt, and within the required latency. NVIDIA’s inference software, co-designed with its GPUs, CPUs, networking, and systems, and supported by a broad open source ecosystem, enhances cost efficiency.
why_it_matters_zh: 了解NVIDIA如何通过软硬件协同优化，降低AI推理代币成本，对构建高效AI生产系统至关重要。
why_it_matters_en: Understanding how NVIDIA optimizes cost per token through integrated hardware and software is crucial for building efficient AI production systems.

title_zh: Jaiveer Singh如何助力机器人及开发者提升速度
title_en: How Jaiveer Singh Is Helping Robots — and Developers — Move Faster
summary_zh: Jaiveer Singh谈论机器人时，重点不是表面上的精彩，而是机器内部的基础设施：机器板卡、让开发者通过机器人摄像头看到画面的软件，以及机器人从演示阶段到实际应用所需的工程工作。作为一名机器人软件工程师，他领导的团队致力于优化这些关键环节。
summary_en: When Jaiveer Singh talks about robots, he focuses on the infrastructure inside machines: the boards, software enabling developers to see through robot cameras, and the engineering needed before a robot moves beyond demos to useful tasks. As a robotics software engineer, he leads the team behind these efforts.
why_it_matters_zh: 理解和优化机器人内部的软件和硬件基础设施，是提升机器人性能和开发效率的关键。Jaiveer Singh的工作帮助机器人更快从实验室走向实际应用，推动机器人技术的进步。
why_it_matters_en: Focusing on the underlying software and hardware infrastructure is essential to enhancing robot performance and developer efficiency. Jaiveer Singh’s work helps robots transition faster from demos to practical uses, advancing robotics technology.

title_zh: 反馈驱动的交互改进机制研究
title_en: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?
summary_zh: 本文研究了自然语言反馈在多轮语言智能体设置中的作用，揭示了改进效果不仅来源于反馈，还可能由于重复尝试、格式修正或额外计算。通过在多个基准任务和开放权重模型上设计受控的师生协议，比较外部反馈、自我反馈与无指导的自我改进，发现有效反馈需超越简单重试，学生对反馈的运用能力是提升的关键瓶颈。
summary_en: This study investigates when natural language feedback produces improvement beyond repeated attempts in multi-turn language agent tasks. Using a controlled student-teacher protocol across various benchmarks and models, the authors compare external feedback, self-feedback, and unguided self-refinement. Findings reveal that feedback-driven improvements depend more on the student's ability to utilize feedback than on the teacher's identity, highlighting that actionable feedback—not mere availability—is crucial for interactive gains.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究详尽分析了反馈改进背后的机制，指出单纯反馈可用性不足以提升表现，强调评估反馈驱动智能体时需对比重复尝试基线，有助于推动更有效的交互式AI系统设计。
why_it_matters_en: This research offers insights into the mechanisms behind feedback-driven improvement, showing that simply having feedback is insufficient; rather, agents' ability to leverage feedback is key. It recommends benchmarking against repeated attempts, advancing the design of more effective interactive AI systems.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 73.

title_zh: 法律领域中的多智能体审议研究
summary_zh: 人工智能在法律领域的应用日益广泛，尤其是基于大型语言模型（LLMs）的多智能体系统（MAD）引起关注。本文提出了两种受法庭程序和法律辩论启发的多智能体框架，探讨其在法律推理任务中的表现。实验表明，多智能体框架在整体表现上与单一模型类似，但能提供明显不同且互补的答案，特别适合于需要多角度批判性思考的问题。研究展示了多智能体在法律人工智能领域的有前景应用。
summary_en: Artificial intelligence is increasingly applied to law, with agentic AI based on large language models (LLMs) drawing interest. This paper investigates multi-agent deliberation (MAD) methods for legal reasoning by introducing two novel frameworks inspired by courtroom procedures and legal argumentation. Experiments on legal and non-legal benchmarks show that these multi-agent frameworks achieve comparable overall performance to baseline LLMs but generate significantly distinct and complementary answers, particularly excelling in questions requiring critical thinking from multiple perspectives. The work highlights the promising potential of multi-agent systems in legal AI.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究首次系统探讨了多智能体在法律推理中的应用，结合法律程序提供创新框架，展示多智能体方法在解决复杂法律问题上的优势，推动法律人工智能的发展。
why_it_matters_en: This research systematically explores multi-agent applications in legal reasoning by introducing innovative frameworks inspired by legal procedures, demonstrating the advantages of multi-agent methods in addressing complex legal issues, thereby advancing AI in the legal domain.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Investigating Multi-Agent Deliberation in Law. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 71.

title_zh: RoPoLL：稳健的大型语言模型评审小组
title_en: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges
summary_zh: LLM Jury（大型语言模型评审团）通过汇报共识分数，成为单一评审替代方案，但其统计行为理解不足。本文基于Huber污染模型，证明在任何正污染下，只要有单个评审表现出偏差（如模式崩溃、谄媚或安全拒绝），PoLL都会产生无界偏差。我们提出RoPoLL，通过引入几何中位数作为稳健均值估计器替代聚合函数，有效抵抗偏差攻击。在13个开权重评审、大型奖励模型基准和多种高达50%污染率的腐败场景下，RoPoLL表现优于PoLL，尤其对抗严重偏差攻击提升约19%，对抗拜占庭攻击更是数量级优势。3评审版RoPoLL以38B参数在HelpSteer-2基准下，在30%双峰随机污染条件下性能超越Mistral-Large-3（675B）1.31倍，实现了18倍的参数效率提升。
summary_en: The LLM Jury, a panel of LLM evaluators reporting consensus scores, offers an alternative to single-judge evaluations but suffers unbounded bias under any positive contamination due to biased judges exhibiting failures like mode collapse, sycophancy, or safety refusal. This work models the jury evaluation under Huber contamination and proposes RoPoLL which retains the LLM panel but replaces the aggregation method with a tuning-free robust mean estimator using the geometric median. RoPoLL achieves a finite-sample error bound matching a minimax lower bound, and significantly outperforms PoLL across various judges, benchmarks, and corruption regimes up to 50%. In particular, a 3-judge RoPoLL committee with 38B parameters surpasses the 675B-parameter Mistral-Large-3 by 1.31x accuracy on HelpSteer-2 under 30% bimodal corruption, demonstrating a substantial parameter efficiency advantage.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究首次严谨分析了LLM评审团在存在偏差和恶意评审时的统计表现，发现传统PoLL方法对偏差极其敏感。通过引入几何中位数的稳健统计方法，RoPoLL有效抵御多种攻击和腐败，显著提升评审结果的可靠性和稳定性。这对LLM评估的公平性和实用性具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research rigorously analyzes statistical limitations of existing LLM juries under biased contaminations and introduces a robust estimator leveraging geometric median to resist diverse corruption types. RoPoLL significantly improves reliability and accuracy in LLM evaluation, ensuring fairer and more stable assessment outcomes. This advancement is vital for trustworthy, large-scale LLM benchmarking and deployment.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 76.

title_zh: 从命题不对称到感知不对称：将摩擦性策略优化扩展到不对称部分信息对话
title_en: From Propositional to Perceptual Asymmetry: Extending Frictive Policy Optimization to Asymmetric Partial Information Dialogue
summary_zh: 本文扩展了摩擦性策略优化（FPO）的方法，将其应用于感知不对称情境，其中对话参与者持有不对称的部分信息，导致相同的指称表达因信息状态不同而产生不同的指代。通过对HCRC MapTask等跨语料库的分析及大型语言模型测试，验证了FPO中摩擦函数在参与者各自信息视野内的有效性，揭示不同地标配置会导致不同的共识失败模式，且少数模糊配置引发大量误解，表明“正确的视角”比全知视角更重要。文章提出了挂起共识状态的子类型分解和兼容性识别的注释改进方案。
summary_en: This paper extends Frictive Policy Optimization (FPO) to scenarios of perceptual asymmetry where dialogue participants hold asymmetric partial information, causing identical referential expressions to denote different things based on each participant's information state. Through cross-corpora analysis and probing with large language models on referentially asymmetric tasks like the HCRC MapTask, it finds FPO's friction functional is valid only within each participant's information horizon. Different landmark configurations produce distinct grounding failures, with some ambiguous cases causing disproportionate misunderstandings that silently diverge. The study highlights that having the 'right perspective' is more impactful than omniscient knowledge. Two annotation refinements are proposed: subtype decomposition of pending grounding states and accommodation-aware alignment classification.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究突破了FPO传统的假设（共享感知上下文），首次系统地考虑了对话中信息不对称的感知差异，揭示了对话误解产生的复杂机制，强调了视角重要性，对构建更鲁棒的对话系统及理解人机沟通具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This work advances the field by moving beyond FPO's assumption of shared perceptual context to systematically address perceptual asymmetry in dialogue with partial information. It uncovers complex mechanisms underlying misunderstandings and highlights the crucial role of perspective in effective communication, offering valuable insights for building more robust dialogue systems and understanding human-machine interaction.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: From Propositional to Perceptual Asymmetry: Extending Frictive Policy Optimization to Asymmetric Partial Information Dialogue. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 63.

title_zh: 基于参考的口语对话系统韵律与节奏评估
title_en: Reference-Based Prosody and Rhythm Evaluation for Spoken Dialogue Systems
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种利用4000多小时英语双人对话数据，构建匹配参考标准，针对基频均值、基频表达力、语速、发音速率、停顿比例和平均停顿时长等指标，评估语音到语音（S2S）人工智能代理的韵律和节奏表现。采用分位数评价协议，将S2S输出与最匹配的人类参考数据进行比较，输出偏离百分位数及异常标志，提升了韵律和节奏评估的解释性和准确性。该方法作为行为合理性检查，补充而非替代感知和用户中心评估。
summary_en: This paper introduces a reference-based evaluation approach for prosody and rhythm in speech-to-speech (S2S) AI systems using over 4000 hours of English dyadic conversation data. It constructs matched reference regimes for metrics like mean F0, F0 expressivity, speech rate, articulation rate, pause ratio, and mean pause duration. A percentile-based protocol compares S2S outputs to matched human references, reporting percentile deviations and out-of-regime flags. This enhances interpretability and accuracy of prosody and rhythm evaluations and serves as a behavioral plausibility check complementing perceptual and user-centered assessments.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统基于整体人类统计的韵律节奏评价难以适应不同模型预测的说话人特质和交互状态，导致评估校准不足。本文基于匹配参考标准的方法，更精确地反映了模型输出的韵律和节奏特征，提升了评价的有效性和解释性。
why_it_matters_en: Conventional prosody and rhythm evaluations relying on pooled human statistics often miscalibrate due to variability in speaker traits and interaction states predicted by models. The matched reference-based approach more accurately captures these characteristics in model outputs, improving evaluation validity and interpretability.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Reference-Based Prosody and Rhythm Evaluation for Spoken Dialogue Systems. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 67.

title_zh: 构建多模态学术论文关键词提取数据集
title_en: Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction
summary_zh: 截至目前，关键词提取任务通常仅依赖文本数据，忽视了图像和音频模态中的视觉和音频特征，导致信息不足及潜在关联被忽视，限制了模型的表现。现有用于关键词提取的多模态数据集非常稀缺，阻碍了研究进展。本文构建了包含1000份样本的学术论文多模态数据集，每份样本包含论文文本、图像、音频及关键词。通过无监督和有监督关键词提取方法，本文比较了论文文本、图像文本和音频文本的提取效果，研究了不同模态信息及其融合对关键词提取的影响。实验结果表明，不同模态文本在模型中具有不同特性，融合多模态文本显著提升了学术论文关键词提取性能。
summary_en: Keyword extraction tasks have traditionally relied exclusively on textual data, neglecting visual and audio features from image and audio modalities, which results in less rich information and overlooked correlations, limiting model performance. Multimodal datasets for this task are scarce, impeding progress. This study builds a multimodal academic paper dataset with 1000 samples, each including paper text, images, audio, and keywords. Using unsupervised and supervised extraction methods, experiments compare keyword extraction performance from paper text, image text, and audio text, investigating different modality contributions and multimodal fusion. Results show that text from varying modalities has distinct characteristics, and concatenating texts from all modalities effectively enhances keyword extraction in academic papers.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究填补了多模态关键词提取数据集的空白，有助于推动相关领域研究。通过整合文本、图像和音频信息，显著提升了关键词提取的准确性，展示了多模态融合在学术信息处理中的潜力。
why_it_matters_en: This work addresses the scarcity of multimodal keyword extraction datasets, facilitating research progress in the field. By integrating text, image, and audio data, it significantly improves keyword extraction accuracy, demonstrating the potential of multimodal fusion for academic information processing.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 63.
